name: test-debt-in-prism

regression_log.reason: Stage 3 critique (critique mode, Full tier) surfaced 5 compound failure modes — CF-1 (containment is theater), CF-2 (token budget hand-waved), CF-3 (runner exit-code interpretation broken), CF-4 (liveness wishful), CF-5 (detection wrong for polyglot). User's threat-model reframe (containment → scope guardrail for first-party tooling) dissolved CF-1 partially (downgrades C1, H8, C9). Remaining: 12 substantive spec changes including agent rename test-debt-reviewer → test-debt-classifier.

describe.confidence_note: Brief is dense and load-bearing decisions are pre-converged; prior-art + empirical feasibility both done; vault context loaded. Main residual uncertainty is whether the v1-vs-future-genericity split on the allowlist hook is right (small; spec stage decision).

specify.confidence_note: Revision 3 trim per /overcomplicated review. User accepted path (a): runner support 6→2 (pytest + bash test.sh), AC consolidation (1-5 → 1), WU13 folded into WU1, Decisions trim. Spec 313 → 299 lines (~5%); WUs 15 → 14; ACs 26 → 22. Confidence rose from 0.78 → 0.82: trimming a deliberately-scoped runner set is more honest than maintaining 6 runners speculatively, and the post-trim spec has a sharper v1 mandate.
preserved_resolutions: 5-category classification (real-issue / test-infrastructure-broken / drift / abandoned / quarantine candidate)
Stage 5.5 position between Quality (5) and Synthesis (6)
Opt-OUT default via SAIL_PRISM_RUN_TESTS=0 env var

challenge.rev2_mode: quick re-Diverge (validation-shaped) + inline polish per user path (a)
challenge.vocabulary_correction: user flagged DISPOSITION_VERIFIED as overstated; relabeled to text_conforms / fact_checked / behaviorally_verified / partial / new_issue / accepted / polished. Saved as feedback_verification_word_precision.md memory.
challenge.phases_skipped_reason: Diverge + Interaction Scan surfaced sufficient evidence for REWORK verdict; Clash/Refine wouldn't dissolve compound failures; Converge done inline by orchestrator given clarity. Will run full pipeline on post-revision re-critique.
challenge.confidence_note: Critique mode (Full tier) was effective — surfaced load-bearing flaws including a misframed threat model that the user reframed inline. Findings catalog in adversarial.md covers 30 items with disposition. After regression to Stage 2, re-Diverge or full re-pipeline will validate fixes.

edge_cases.mode: quick Diverge x3 (validation-shaped); Interaction Scan inline by orchestrator
edge_cases.verdict_note: Most findings dissolved under rev3 trim (runner-multiplication was the underlying cluster). Remaining items deferred to v2 with rationale.

premortem.regression_decision: option [2] — note + inline-fix; PM2/PM4/PM7 polished; PM1/PM5/PM8 deferred
premortem.confidence_note: Pre-mortem found 2 NEW critical/high findings; one verified factually (PM2 install.sh merge gap). User chose inline-fix path — added runtime self-probe (AC23) + stale-state recovery (AC24) + install detection (WU14). PM1 (opt-out default) challenged but user stood by earlier decision. PM5+PM8 scope expansions deferred to v2.

review.skip_reason: Diminishing returns — spec absorbed substantial adversarial coverage already (full Stage 3 critique + Stage 3 re-Diverge + Stage 4 Edge Cases Diverge + Stage 4.5 pre-mortem + /overcomplicated trim). Each subsequent review pass surfaced smaller items. External review (/gpt-review) cost > marginal value at this point. User opted (a) — skip + proceed to Stage 6 (spec-to-tests, which is genuinely new work).

manual_smoke_checks: T10.3 ambient PreToolUse hooks for subagents — manual per prior-art.md
outside_scope_acknowledged: perf tests advisory not blocking on slow envs
test.confidence_note: 30 spec-blind tests across 11 categories cover all 24 ACs + 7 preservation invariants + 5 failure modes. Helper functions (simulate_* family) need to be implemented as part of WU9; documented in tests.md. Tests should fail initially (no impl) and pass when impl matches spec — that's the contract. AC10 noted as manual smoke check (can't fully automate without Claude Code subprocess).

execute.confidence_note: All 14 WUs landed; test.sh 132 passed/0 failed/3 warnings; behavioral-smoke.sh extension lands hook-exec mode and 7 hook fixtures pass; hook smoke-tested across 8 stdin shapes; one inline test-grep fix needed (table-format vs rc= notation); WU12 behavioral subagent verification deferred to user post-restart per wu12-verification.md

debrief.spec_delta_summary: 1 formal regression (rev1→rev2 via CF-1 threat-model reframe) + 2 inline polish passes (rev2-polish, rev3 /overcomplicated trim). 14 WUs, 24 ACs, PASS verdict.
deferred_items: WU12 behavioral subagent-dispatch verification — see wu12-verification.md
AC11 behavioral word-count measurement — same constraint as WU12
agents/README.md row for test-debt-classifier — file does not exist (spec drift)
v2 deferred runners: bun, cargo, go, npm, jest
Subagent abandoned-mid-run state recovery beyond AC24 (M1 partial)
Log file rotation in wizard dirs (v2)

discoveries: evals.json schema mismatch resolved via Option A: extended behavioral-smoke.sh with hook-execution mode (~75 lines) preserving existing fixture mode
README.md hook count was already 20 (one ahead of .claude/CLAUDE.md stale claim of 19); WU11 update math had to handle stale baseline + new addition
agents/README.md does not exist; spec WU11 referenced nonexistent target; surfaced in preflight, skipped
install.sh has no hardcoded count claims; spec WU11 12→13/18→19 update was unnecessary
PM2 condition reachable on real install: ~/.claude/settings.json does not auto-merge new hook entry; validates AC23 motivation
Spec inconsistency: per-runner exit-code prose uses rc=1 notation but WU1 table uses bare numbers (| 1 |); test.sh structural check tightened to match table-row format
commands/prism.md grew ~210 lines from Stage 5.5 insertion; future cross-references should re-verify line numbers
Spec rev3 trim renumbered WUs but kept the WU13 ID for different content; cross-references say former-WU13 vs current-WU13 — confusing

prior_art_gate.note: No tooling fits; industry has flaky-detection only. Build with informed borrowing (retry-based detection + govuk-rfcs/rfc-069 severity-axis). Hook feasibility empirically confirmed.

load_bearing_context.user_rule: feedback_preexisting_test_debt — stop dismissing test failures; fix or flag for cleanup. Standing instruction motivating this blueprint.
load_bearing_context.prism_context_risk: Prism orchestrator context exhaustion is a known issue (high-severity finding). Adding Stage 5.5 increases this risk. Token budget design is load-bearing.
load_bearing_context.predecessor_lessons: Anti-pattern-catalog debrief: pre-impl empirical gating works; spec-blind tests with hard thresholds beat fuzzy contracts; settings.local.json hooks are session-cached (already burned us once, accounted for).
load_bearing_context.threat_model_reframe: User reframed 'containment' (security boundary) as 'scope guardrail' (catches agent confusion). Recorded as feedback memory; applies to first-party tooling generally. Dissolved CF-1 critical finding from rev1 critique.

research_brief_status: no-formal-research.md; equivalent context provided by (a) prior-session brief in memory project_test_debt_design.md, (b) prior-art.md generated this session with empirical hook-feasibility verdict, (c) vault notes consulted above
